allocator.rationale: Tree indices, orders, alignment, and caller-owned backing storage are bounded before pointer derivation.

bsp-concurrency.rationale: The interrupt queue has one producer, one consumer, release/acquire publication, and bounded overflow accounting.

bsp-hardware.rationale: Unsafe operations are confined to feature-selected MCU MMIO, typed peripheral ownership, linker-owned memory, exception capture, or explicitly bounded destructive storage roles.
verification: per-MCU HIL and MPU negative access

bsp-tms570-hardware.rationale: Unsafe operations are confined to revision-B TMS570LC4357 descriptor storage, volatile EMAC words, private retained-record publication, and target-only exception assembly with fixed layout, alignment, ownership, byte-order, banked-state, reset, and barrier contracts; raw pointers are not exposed to applications.
verification: TMS570 descriptor and retained-record host tests
TMS570 startup/exception linker and disassembly assertions

can-posix-ffi.rationale: Socket descriptors, sockaddr layouts, frame buffers, lengths, and return codes are validated at the libc boundary.

concurrency.rationale: SPSC handles are uniquely split and release/acquire cursors publish disjoint initialized slots.

container.rationale: Raw storage initialization, movement, destruction, pinning, and intrusive-link ownership follow the live-length and capacity invariants.

io-concurrency.rationale: Published cursors separate writer-owned and reader-owned initialized byte regions with bounded wraparound.
